TW202411896A: The federated learning system includes a moderator and a plurality of client devices. Each client device performs a method for verifying model update as follows: receiving a hash function and a general model; training a client model according to the general model and raw data; computing a difference between the general model and the client model as an update parameter, sending the update parameter to the moderator so that the moderator receives a plurality of update parameters; inputting the update parameter to the hash function to generate a hash value; sending the hash value to other client devices, thereby receiving a plurality of hash values; summing all hash values to generate a trust value; receiving an aggregation parameter computed according to the update parameters by the moderator; inputting the aggregation parameter to the hash function to generate a to-be-verified value; and updating the client model according to the aggregation parameter when the to-be-verified value equals the trust value.
TW202321822A: A method of manufacturing a semiconductor device includes forming a first tone resist layer over an underlayer. The first tone resist layer is pattern to form a first pattern exposing a portion of the underlayer. The first pattern is extended into the underlayer, and the first tone resist layer is removed. A second tone resist layer is formed over the underlayer, wherein the second tone is opposite the first tone. The second tone resist layer is patterned to form a second pattern exposing another portion of the underlayer. The second pattern is extended into underlayer, and the second tone resist layer is removed.
TWI818706B: A method of manufacturing a semiconductor device includes forming a first tone resist layer over an underlayer. TWI871528B: The present disclosure provides a measurement method and a lithography process control system. The lithography process control system is configured to perform the following steps. Deviation between an edge critical dimension and a center critical dimension of each shot areas is calculated. Whether the deviation of each shot areas is in a deviation range is determined. If the deviation in each shot areas is in the deviation range, a deviation value along a main deviation direction in one of the shot areas is evaluated. Parameters of a photomask are adjusted according to the deviation value along the main deviation direction.
TW202526521A: The present disclosure provides a measurement method and a lithography process control system. The lithography process control system is configured to perform the following steps. Deviation between an edge critical dimension and a center critical dimension of each shot area on a wafer is calculated. Whether the deviation of each shot area is in a deviation range is determined. If the deviation in each shot area is in the deviation range, a deviation value along a main deviation direction in one of the shot areas is evaluated. Parameters of a photomask are adjusted according to the deviation value along the main deviation direction.

A photo mask includes a plurality of device features, a first assist feature, and a second assist feature. The device features are in a patterning region of a device region. The first assist feature are in the patterning region and adjacent to the device features. The first assist feature is for correcting an optical proximity effect in a photolithography process. The second assist feature is in a non-patterning region of the device region. The second assist feature is a sub-resolution correction feature, and a first distance between the second assist feature and one of the device features closest to the second assist feature is greater than a second distance between adjacent two of the device 
features.
TW202312178A: Performing a built-in self-test (BIST) on a memory macro includes generating a plurality of input vectors such that at least one input vector of the plurality of input vectors is transmitted to the memory macro in each of a plurality of cycles, receiving in each of the plurality of cycles, an output data from the memory macro. The output data is generated by the memory macro in response to processing the at least one input vector. The BIST also includes comparing the output data in each of the plurality of cycles with a signature value and determining whether the memory macro is normal or faulty based upon the comparison.
TW202318410A: Disclosed herein are related to a memory array including a set of memory cells and a set of switches to configure the set of memory cells. In one aspect, each switch is connected between a corresponding local line and a corresponding subset of memory cells. The local clines may be connected to a global line. Local lines may be metal rails, for example, local bit lines or local select lines. A global line may be a metal rail, for example, a global bit line or a global select line. A switch may be enabled or disabled to electrically couple a controller to a selected subset of memory cells through the global line. Accordingly, the set of memory cells can be configured through the global line rather than a number of metal rails to achieve area efficiency.
TWI825931B: A memory device and a method for operating the same are provided. In an erase operation, a switch voltage is applied to at least one of a string select line or a ground select line of a selected sub-block of a selected block, a gate control voltage is applied to selected word lines of the selected sub-block, and an erase voltage is applied to bit lines and a common source line of the selected sub-block. The switch voltage is smaller than the erase voltage. The gate control voltage is smaller than the switch voltage and the erase voltage.
TW202410032A: A memory device and a method for operating the same are provided. The switch voltage is smaller than the erase voltage. The gate control voltage is smaller than the switch voltage and the erase voltage.
TW202320068A: A method for fabricating a semiconductor device includes the steps of forming a magnetic tunneling junction (MTJ) on a substrate, forming a first inter-metal dielectric (IMD) layer on the MTJ, removing part of the first IMD layer to form a damaged layer on the MTJ and a trench exposing the damaged layer, performing a ultraviolet (UV) curing process on the damaged layer, and then conducting a planarizing process to remove the damaged layer and part of the first IMD layer.
TWI847218B: A text image splitting system includes: a database storing an initial text data, a first numerical data, a second numerical data and a plurality of original recognized text data, wherein the initial text data includes a first text data and a second text data; and a server accessing the database, wherein the server receives an initial image data, wherein the initial image data includes a first text image data and a second text image data; wherein the server 120 determines whether the initial image data conforms to the initial text data at least by using the first text data, the second text data, the first text image data, the second text image data, the first numerical data, the second numerical data and the plurality of original recognized text data.
TWI882243B: A semiconductor package structure includes a first die, a second die disposed on the first die, and a bonding pad structure. The first die includes a semiconductor substrate, an interconnect structure disposed on the first semiconductor substrate, a passivation layer disposed on the interconnect structure, and a test pad disposed on the passivation layer. The test pad includes a contact region that extends through the passivation layer and electrically contacts the interconnect structure, and a bonding recess that overlaps with the contact region in a vertical direction perpendicular to a plane of the first semiconductor substrate. The bonding pad structure electrically connects the first die and the second die and directly contacts at least a portion of the bonding recess.
TW202324416A: The disclosure provides a semiconductor memory device capable of automatically restoring writing interrupted by a momentary stop or fluctuation of a power supply voltage. The non-volatile memory of the disclosure includes a memory cell array formed with an NOR type array and a resistance variable array. When the power supply voltage drops to the power-off level during writing to the NOR array, the read/write control unit writes unwritten data into the variable resistance array. After that, when the power supply voltage connecting is detected, the read/write control unit reads the unwritten data from the variable resistance array and writes the unwritten data to the NOR array. In this way, the interrupted writing is restored.
TWI814531B: The disclosure provides a semiconductor memory device capable of automatically restoring writing interrupted by a momentary stop or fluctuation of a power supply voltage. The non-volatile memory of the disclosure includes a memory cell array formed with an NOR type array and a resistance variable array. After that, when the power supply voltage connecting is detected, the read/write control unit reads the unwritten data from the variable resistance array and writes the unwritten data to the NOR array. In this way, the interrupted writing is restored.
TWI825893B: The disclosure provides an image processing method and a display device. The image processing method is suitable for the display device. The display device includes a scaler IC and a panel module. The image processing method includes: when the scaler IC receives a notification signal from a source to switch the display format, the scaler IC stores a current image frame in a memory. The scaler IC provides the current image frame to the panel module, so that the panel module displays the current image frame. The scaler IC is re-handshaking with the source and the panel module to receive a new image frame provided by the source, and the scaler IC transmits the new image frame to the panel module, so that the panel module displays the new image frame.
TW202408213A: The disclosure provides an image processing method and a display device. The image processing method is suitable for the display device. TWI807942B:
A magnetoresistive random access memory (MRAM) device is provided. The MRAM 
device includes a main magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ) array comprising a plurality of memory cells configured to store memory data and a reference MTJ array comprising a plurality of reference cells having MTJ structures. The MRAM device further includes a controller operatively associated with the main MTJ array and the reference MTJ array. The controller is configured to receive a gross resistance of the reference MTJ array being related to a strength of an external magnetic field, determine whether the external magnetic field is fatal based on the received gross resistance of the reference MTJ array and a pre-determined threshold, and provide notification indicating that the memory data stored in the main MTJ array is untrustworthy if it is determined that the external magnetic field around the MRAM device is fatal.

TW202347292A: A touch detection method for a touch and display driving circuit includes transmitting a first touch pulse signal of a first frame and transmitting a first touch pulse signal of a second frame. TW202305734A: Some aspects of the present application provide a scene detection module in a computing device that determines a quality score from a graphic command output for a first image. As a result, the computing device may be improved by saving system resources including memory bandwidth, processing power for apps or instances, without negatively affecting a user's visual perception of the scene.

TWI827137B:
A memory device for in-memory search, the memory device includes a plurality of memory cells, and each of the memory cells stores a stored data and receives a search data, including a first transistor and a second transistor. The first transistor has a first threshold voltage and receives a first gate bias. The second transistor is connected to the first transistor, and the second transistor has a second threshold voltage and receives a second gate bias. threshold voltage, and the search data is encoded according to the first gate bias and the second gate bias. There is a mismatch distance between the stored data and the search data. An output current generated by each of the memory cells is related to the mismatch distance.

TWI827136B: The disclosure provides a semiconductor storage device and a reading method, which can achieve high-speed processing time for error detection and correction and achieve miniaturization. The flash memory (100) of the disclosure has a NAND chip (200) and an ECC chip (300). NAND chip (200) has: memory cell array; page buffer/sensing circuit, including latch (L1) and latch (L2); and dedicated input and output terminals (220), which can be used with ECC chip (300) data transmission between. The latch (L1) contains the cache (C0, C1), and the latch (L2) only contains the cache (C1). The data in the cache (C0) of the latch (L1) and the data in the cache (C1) of the latch (L2) are transferred to the ECC chip (300), in response to the output of the first address from the ECC chip (300), read the next page from the memory cell array, and the read data is held in the latch (L1).
TWI827138B: The disclosure provides a semiconductor storage device, which can shorten the processing time for error detection and correction. The flash memory (100) of the present invention has a NAND chip (200) and an ECC chip (300). The NAND chip (200) has dedicated input and output terminals (220) which can transmit data with the ECC chip (300), and the ECC chip (300) has a dedicated input and output terminal (320) which can transmit data with the NAND chip (200).When reading in the NAND chip (200), the NAND chip (200) transmits the read data containing the parity data to the ECC chip (300) through the dedicated input and output terminals (220). The ECC chip (300) detect and correct errors in the read data base on the parity data, and the corrected data is transmitted to the controller (400) through the input and output terminals (330).

TW202309668A: A reticle carrier is configured to quickly discharge the residual charge on a reticle so as to prevent particles in the reticle carrier from being attracted to or transferred to the reticle. The reticle carrier may be configured to provide reduced capacitance between an inner baseplate of the reticle carrier and the reticle. The reduction in capacitance may reduce the resistance-capacitance (RC) time constant for discharging the residual charge on the reticle, which may increase the discharge speed for discharging the residual charge through support pins of the reticle carrier. The increase in discharge speed may reduce the likelihood that an electrostatic force in the reticle carrier may attract particles in the reticle carrier to the reticle. This may reduce pattern defects transferred to substrates that are patterned using the reticle, may increase semiconductor device manufacturing quality and yield.

TW202320070A: The invention provides a dynamic random access memory (DRAM) and an operation method thereof. The DRAM includes a memory cell array, a temperature sensor, and a refresh logic circuit. In a temperature-controlled refresh mode, the refresh logic circuit correspondingly adjusts the number of a plurality of tRAS periods in the tRFC according to a temperature sensing result of the temperature sensor. TWI796906B: A light curing device includes a supporting platform, a photomask and an ultraviolet light source. The supporting platform is configured to support a display device. TW202326659A: A display apparatus is provided, which includes a display panel and a display controller. The display controller is configured to receive a video signal from a host, and displays the video signal on the display panel. The display controller calculates an image average brightness of each image in the video signal. When an absolute different between a first image average brightness of a current image in the video signal and a second image average brightness of a previous in the video signal is less than a predetermined value, the display controller activates a timer to obtain a timer value. When the timer value reaches a first time period, the display controller lowers brightness of each pixel in the current image by a first ratio. When the timer value reaches a second timer period, the display controller lowers brightness of each pixel in the current image by a second ratio, wherein the second time period is longer than the first time period, and the second ratio is higher than the first ratio.

TW202326745A: An electronic test system includes a reader, a memory, and a processor. The reader is configured to read and write a plurality of data. TW202326743A: A read disturb checking method, a memory storage device and a memory control circuit unit are provided. TW202326698A: A multi-screen display device and an operation method thereof are provided. The multi-screen display device includes a first screen, a second screen, a displacement sensor, and a processing circuit. The displacement sensor is used to detect a relative position of the second screen to the first screen, and output at least one sensing signal related to the relative position. The processing circuit is coupled to the first screen, the second screen and the displacement sensor. The processing circuit adjusts a display resolution of the second screen according to the at least one sensing signal.
TW202244625A: A reticle is pre-heated prior to an exposure operation of a semiconductor substrate lot to reduce substrate to substrate temperature variations of the reticle in the exposure operation. The reticle may be pre-heated while being stored in a reticle storage slot, while being transferred from the reticle storage slot to a reticle stage of an exposure tool, and/or in another location prior to being secured to the reticle stage for the exposure operation. In this way, the reduction in temperature variation of the reticle in the exposure operation provided by pre-heating the reticle may reduce overlay deltas and misalignment for the semiconductor substrates that are processed in the exposure operation. This increases overlay performance, increases yield of the exposure tool, and increases semiconductor device quality. Moreover, pre-heating the reticle prior to securing the reticle to the reticle stage for the exposure operation reduces and/or minimizes the impact that the pre-heating has on throughput and processing times of the exposure tool.
TW202242568A: A method for forming a semiconductor device is provided. The method for forming a semiconductor device is provided. The method includes coating a photoresist film over a target layer; performing a lithography process to pattern the photoresist film into a photoresist layer; performing a directional ion bombardment process to the photoresist layer, such that a carbon atomic concentration in the photoresist layer is increased; and etching the target layer using the photoresist layer as an etch mask.
TW202327343A: A conference device includes an image capturing unit, a communication unit and a processor. The processor is configured to transmit certification information of the first electronic device to obtain a cursor operation authority. The processor is configured to receive a real-time visual signal comprising a real-time state generated by applying an operation instruction message matching the cursor operation authority to a representation cursor of the certification information and comprising a real-time state generated by applying the operation instruction message to a representation cursor represented a remote participant of the online conference.

TW202324417A: A solid state disk, a data transmitting method and an intermediary controller thereof are provided. TWI786947B: The present application provides an anti-fuse one-time programmable (OTP) memory array and a manufacturing method of the anti-fuse one-time programmable (OTP) memory array. A region in which a pair of programming word line and read word line are intersected with one of the active areas defines a unit cell in the memory array. TW202320076A: A memory time-division control device controls a content addressable memory (CAM) cell array of a CAM in a time-division manner to reduce a peak current and mitigate the problems of electromigration and voltage variation. TW202318881A: The present invention is a method for controlling augmented reality content playback and system thereof. A paragraph description file for the content of the augmented reality video is configured in the augmented reality video to generate each paragraph of the film and its audio-visual characteristics. When the augmented reality video is played by an electronic device as the augmented reality content, whether the scene situation meets the recorded characteristics can be compared, and compare results can be used for controlling the playback progress of the content of the augmented reality video. In addition, recording changes in the playback progress due to control actions can also be used as a reference for video content adjustment. Therefore, the present invention allows the user to focus on practical operations without distracting control of the playback progress of the augmented reality video, so that better augmented reality learning operations and efficiency are provided. The present invention also provides a computer-readable medium for executing the method of the present invention.

TW202318867A: The disclosure provides an eye tracking method and an eye tracking device. The method includes: obtaining a reference interpupillary distance value; taking images of a user of a 3D display, and finding a first eye pixel coordinate corresponding to a first eye of the user and a second eye pixel coordinate corresponding to a second eye of the user in each image; detecting a first and a second eye spatial coordinates of the first and the second eyes, and determining projection coordinates based on the first eye spatial coordinate, the second eye spatial coordinate, and optical parameters of image capturing elements; determining an optimization condition related to the first and second eye spatial coordinates based on the first and second eye pixel coordinates, the projection coordinates, and the reference interpupillary distance value of each image; and optimizing the first and second eye spatial coordinates based on the optimization condition.

updates the stored read delay time, and repeats to read and verify the test data until the verification result showing the two data are the same.

TWI762257B:
The present invention is a method for recommending the viewing directions in a virtual reality video. During the process of playing a video information on a virtual reality playback device and displaying the virtual reality video by a head-mounted display. A recommended angle from the video information and a viewing angle from the head-mounted display are obtained by the virtual reality playback device. A recommended direction is calculated after comparing the recommended angle and the viewing angle, and the recommended direction 
is displayed in the virtual reality video. Therefore, the present invention can prompt the direction of the recommended screen when the user watches the virtual reality video, thereby quickly and precisely providing the user with a better viewing effect. A computer-readable medium for implementing a method for recommending viewing directions in a virtual reality movie is further provided in the present invention.

When the television device is activated, the television control circuit performs a menu-loading sequence that loads one of the television menu settings from the memory. The television control circuit parses the television menu setting and obtains control tags and the corresponding setting parameters. A television menu is initiated and displayed on the television device based on the control tags and the setting parameters.

TW202230374A: An operation method of a memory device is provided. The memory device includes a P-type well, a common source line, a memory array, a plurality of word lines, and a serial selection line, a ground selection line, and at least one bit line. The word lines include a first word line and a second word line that are programmed and not adjacent to each other. The operation method includes the following steps. A read voltage is applied to a selected word line. A pass voltage is applied to unselected word lines, and the read voltage is less than the pass voltage. During a period when the pass voltage ramps down to a lower level before the end of a read operation, a channel potential of the memory string is down-coupled, a hole current is injected to flow from the P-type well to the memory string to neutralize the channel voltage in advance.

The present invention provides a receiver including a sampling circuit, a data sampling point selection circuit and a determination circuit. The sampling circuit is configured to use a clock signal to sample an input signal to generate a sampled signal, wherein a frequency of the clock signal is higher than a frequency of the input signal. The data sampling point selection circuit is configured to filter start point data to generate a filtered start point data, and to generate a data sampling point by adding an offset to the filtered start point data, wherein the start point data corresponds to a time point that a sampled value of sampled signal starts to change. circuit is configured to refer to a sampled value corresponding to the data sampling point in the sampled signal to determine a logical value of a digital output signal corresponding to the input signal.

TWI746336B: A Storage device management method for a storage device is provided. TW202137184A: A display device and a driving protection method thereof are provided. The display device includes a timing controller and a source driver. TWI759988B:
Various embodiments of the present disclosure are directed 
towards a method for forming a semiconductor structure and a processing system. The method includes forming a plurality of upper alignment marks on a semiconductor wafer. A plurality of lower alignment marks is formed on a handle wafer and correspond to the upper alignment marks. The semiconductor wafer is bonded to the handle wafer such that centers of the upper alignment marks are laterally offset from centers of corresponding lower alignment 
marks. An overlay (OVL) shift is measured between the handle wafer and the semiconductor wafer by detecting the plurality of upper alignment marks and the plurality of lower alignment marks. A photolithography process is performed by a photolithography tool to partially form an integrated circuit (IC) structure over the semiconductor wafer. During the photolithography process the photolithography tool is compensatively aligned according to the OVL shift.

During the photolithography process the photolithography tool is compensatively aligned according to the OVL shift.
TWI772988B: A system for verifying integrity of content of an integrated circuit's registers, the system being operative in conjunction with an integrated circuit including at least one memory, at least one processor, and a multiplicity of registers, the system comprising register content verification logic configured, when in a first mode aka " Study Mode", to read at least some of the registers' content, to compute a first hash on the content, and to store the first hash thereby to provide an up-to-date reference hash, and, at least on occasion, when in a second mode aka " Verify Mode ", to compute at least one second hash on the content, to compare the second hash to the reference hash and, accordingly, to provide a content verification output (aka "fault detection" output) indicative of whether the reference and second hashes are equal.

TW202113458A: The present disclosure describes a method of patterning a semiconductor wafer using extreme ultraviolet lithography (EUVL). The method includes receiving an EUVL mask that includes a substrate having a low temperature expansion material, a reflective multilayer over the substrate, a capping layer over the reflective multilayer, and an absorber layer over the capping layer. The method further includes patterning the absorber layer to form a trench on the EUVL mask, wherein the trench has a first width above a target width. The method further includes treating the EUVL mask with oxygen plasma to reduce the trench to a second width, wherein the second width is below the target width. The method may also include treating the EUVL mask with nitrogen plasma to protect the capping layer, wherein the treating of the EUVL mask with the nitrogen plasma expands the trench to a third width at the target width.

In some embodiments, the present disclosure relates to a method of operating a phase change memory cell, which includes writing a first data state and a second data state to the phase change memory cell. To write the first data state, a phase change material (PCM) is heated to a melting point of the PCM, and then cooled to an ambient temperature below the melting point of the PCM over a first predetermined time period, thereby solidifying the PCM to correspond to the first data state. To write the second data state, the PCM is heated to the melting point of the PCM, and then cooled to the ambient temperature over a second predetermined cooling time period, thereby solidifying the PCM to correspond to the second data state. .

TW202109175A: A method for fabricating a photomask is provided. The method includes several operations. The plurality of second radiation shots are emitted along a first direction. A roughness of a boundary of the first beta pattern along the first direction is compared to a roughness of a boundary of the reference pattern along the first direction from a top view perspective. TWI726622B: A semiconductor device capable of suppressing generation of noise caused by EMI is provided. The flash memory (100) includes a memory cell array (110), a clock generator (200), a readout part, an input/output circuit (120), an overlap detecting part (330) and a clock control part (210). The clock generator (200) generates an internal clock signal (InCLK). The readout part reads data from a selected memory cell of the memory cell array (110) using the internal clock signal (InCLK). The input/output circuit (150) outputs the read data using an external clock signal (ExCLK) supplied from outside. The overlap detecting unit (330) detects a period during which a rising edge of the internal clock signal (InCLK) overlaps with a rising edge of the external clock signal (ExCLK). The clock control part (210) controls a timing of the internal clock signal (InCLK) in response to the detected overlap period.

TW202032554A: A pseudo static random access memory including a memory controller, a plurality of memory chips and an information storing device is provided. The memory chips transmit a plurality of read/write data strobe signals to the memory controller by using the same bus. No matter a self refresh collision happens in the memory chips, when the memory chips perform a read operation, read latency of the memory chips is set to be a fixed period that a self refresh operation is allowed being finished. The fixed period is larger than an initial latency. The information storing device is configured to store an information which defines the fixed period. The read/write data strobe signal indicates whether the self refresh collision happens in the memory chips, and a level of the read/write data strobe signal is constant during the read latency. A method for operating a pseudo static random access memory is also provided.

TW202127144A: A method for fabricating a semiconductor device includes steps as follows: First, a substrate is provided and a silicon-containing anti-reflection material is coated on the substrate. The silicon-containing anti-reflection material s then cured with a first temperature ranging from 90℃ to 200℃ to form an anti-reflection layer. A photoresist layer is formed on the anti-reflection layer , and an exposure process is performed on the photoresist layer to convert a portion of the photoresist layer into a sacrificial region. The sacrificial region is removed by a developing solution containing tetramethylammonium hydroxide, and a part of the anti-reflection layer located under the sacrificial region is removed at the same time. The remaining antireflection layer is removed by a first solvent.

TW202029063A: A system includes an accelerator to accelerate the computations of nonlinear math functions. The accelerator includes a set of first evaluators and a set of second evaluators. Each of the first evaluators and the second evaluators is a fixed-function hardware circuit specialized for evaluating a respective nonlinear function. The system further includes a processor which decodes a math instruction in an instruction set, sends to the accelerator a signal identifying a nonlinear function corresponding to the math instruction, and receives an output of the nonlinear function from the accelerator. According to the signal, the accelerator evaluates the nonlinear function using one of the first evaluators. When the signal identifies the nonlinear function as a composite function, the accelerator additionally uses one of second evaluators on an output of the one first evaluator.

TWI732410B:
This invention provides a system and method for automatic reporting of detecting a set-top box abnormally, includes a detector with abnormal 
control module, Log storage module, and network connection module, and also a server with corresponded network connection module, abnormal control module, Log storage module, database, analysis module, searching interface, abnormal message generated module, and network transfer module. When a detector detects a set-top box occurring abnormal problem, the abnormal message can be caught in time and transfer to the server. After checking the integrity of the abnormal message to be analyzed, the server will send the abnormal message to related crews, or feedback the solution of the abnormal message directly. The system and method for automatic reporting of detecting a set-top box abnormally can automatically and immediately transfer the solution of the abnormal message if the abnormal message is known, or transfer to the related crews to handle. This can make the related crew realize the problem with key points and solutions thereof before the customer responds the problem, and further improve the efficiency of solving the abnormal message and the quality of the set-top box.

TWI701674B: A memory apparatus and a data reading method thereof are provided. In the method, a plurality of memory cells of the memory apparatus are read to obtain read data, in which a threshold voltage of each memory cell is sensed, and respectively compared with a first reference voltage and a second reference voltage to determine bit values. The first reference voltage and the second reference voltage are used to distinguish different states of the memory cell and the second reference voltage is larger than the first reference voltage. The bit values of the memory cells having the threshold voltage between the first reference voltage and the second reference voltage in the read data are gradually changed and a syndrome of the changed read data is calculated. The read data is corrected according to values of the syndrome.

TW202126052A: The disclosure provides a Game Raiders video recommendation system, a raider providing device and method thereof, which adapt to communicating with a data server. The processing module of the raider providing device extracts a segment from the video and audio information of the game unit as a video and audio information based on the driving signal of the input device, and analyzes the video and audio information to generate a video feature. The data server searches for at least one video raider information according to the video feature, and the processing module generates the video raider information list based on the video raider information. The processing module selects one from the video raider information list according to the control signal of the input device, and the corresponding one in the selected video raider information list is a raider image to be displayed, and finally displays the video raider information list or the raider image to be displayed. Based on this, the function of automatically recommending the video and audio raider information required by the user in the game screen is achieved.

TW202123094A: The present invention is a method for improving the accuracy of classification problems in neural networks and computer readable storage medium. The method deals with complex nonlinear problems through the characteristics of cubic function. The characteristic is that when the input value is between ±2, the output value changes little, and if the input value exceeds this range, as the value increases or decreases, the increase or decrease of the output value will have a significant difference. The result is passed to the next neuron or the output layer, the next neuron or output will be more significant. Therefore, the present invention improve the passivation problem of prior art, and can achieve higher accuracy faster in fewer training times, and can avoid the problem of the gradient vanishing that may occur when the hidden layer of the neural network is increased. Further, the whole effectiveness and performance could be improved.
